plugins.name: Preprocessors
plugins.description: A preprocessor is the plugin responsible for preparing a support file or a test file for the browser. By default, Cypress comes packaged with webpack preprocessor already installed. Read the Preprocessors API docs to learn more.

plugins.name: Webpack
plugins.description: Watches and bundles your spec files via webpack.

plugins.name: @bahmutov/cypress-esbuild-preprocessor
plugins.description: Bundle Cypress specs using esbuild.

plugins.name: Cucumber
plugins.description: Run Cucumber/Gherkin-syntax specs with Cypress.

plugins.name: cypress-markdown-preprocessor
plugins.description: Cypress preprocessor for extracting tests from Markdown files.

plugins.name: cypress-vite
plugins.description: Cypress preprocessor for running specs using Vite.

plugins.name: Component Testing
plugins.description: Loading and mounting components from various frameworks.

plugins.name: Cypress Angular
plugins.description: Test Angular components using Cypress Test Runner. This package is bundled with the cypress package and does not need to be installed separately, unless a specific version is desired. See the Angular Component Testing Docs for mounting Angular components.

plugins.name: Cypress React
plugins.description: Test React components using Cypress Test Runner. This package is bundled with the cypress package and does not need to be installed separately, unless a specific version is desired. See the React Component Testing Docs for mounting React components.

plugins.name: Cypress Svelte
plugins.description: Test Svelte components using Cypress Test Runner. This package is bundled with the cypress package and should not need to be installed separately. See the Svelte Component Testing Docs for mounting Svelte components.

plugins.name: Cypress Vue
plugins.description: Test Vue 3 components using Cypress Test Runner. This package is bundled with the cypress package and should not need to be installed separately. See the Vue Component Testing Docs for mounting Vue components.

plugins.name: cypress-ct-custom-devserver
plugins.description: Simplified API for creating custom dev servers for Cypress.

plugins.name: cypress-devserver-esbuild
plugins.description: Build Cypress Component Tests using esbuild.

plugins.name: cypress-rspack-dev-server
plugins.description: Build Cypress Component Tests using Rspack.

plugins.name: AI Tooling
plugins.description: Connect AI coding assistants and agents to Cypress so they write, understand, and debug tests more reliably. See our Cypress AI Skills guide to get started.

plugins.name: Cypress MCP
plugins.description: Model Context Protocol server that connects AI coding assistants directly to Cypress Cloud, giving them access to your application's health and test stability data.

plugins.name: cypress-author
plugins.description: AI skill that improves how AI tools create, update, and fix Cypress end-to-end and component tests.

plugins.name: cypress-explain
plugins.description: AI skill that helps AI tools understand, describe, and critique existing Cypress tests — surfacing weak selectors and flaky assertions.

plugins.name: cypress-docs
plugins.description: AI skill that lets agents research and retrieve Cypress documentation more efficiently and reliably.

plugins.name: Continuous Integration & Parallelization
plugins.description: Run Cypress in CI and split work across machines to keep test runs fast.

plugins.name: CircleCI Cypress Orb
plugins.description: Cypress Orb for CircleCI configuration to correctly install, cache and run Cypress.io tests in CircleCI with very little effort.

plugins.name: Cypress GitHub Actions
plugins.description: GitHub Action for running Cypress end-to-end and component tests. Includes npm, pnpm and Yarn installation, custom caching and lots of configuration options.

plugins.name: Docker
plugins.description: Docker images providing all the dependencies to run Cypress in CI including browsers.

plugins.name: cypress-load-balancer
plugins.description: A simple load balancer for Cypress tests. Use this for parallelizing jobs across CI/CD solutions or locally in separate processes.

plugins.name: cypress-repeat
plugins.description: Run Cypress multiple times in a row, great at finding test flake.

plugins.name: Developer Tooling
plugins.description: Improve the day-to-day developer experience with editor support, linting, type definitions, and test-authoring helpers.

plugins.name: Cypress Chrome Recorder
plugins.description: Command-line tool to convert Google Chrome DevTools Recorder exports into Cypress tests.

plugins.name: Cypress Chrome Recorder Extension
plugins.description: Chrome DevTools extension that exports Cypress tests directly from the Recorder panel — no CLI needed.

plugins.name: eslint-plugin-cypress
plugins.description: Official ESLint plugin that configures Cypress and Mocha globals and enforces best-practice rules to prevent flaky tests and common Cypress anti-patterns — catching issues in-editor before CI runs.

plugins.name: TypeScript
plugins.description: Official TypeScript definitions for the Cypress API.

plugins.name: cypress-codegen
plugins.description: A Cypress plugin which automatically adds and enables IntelliSense for custom commands

plugins.name: cypress-runner-themes
plugins.description: Alternative themes for the Cypress Test Runner.

plugins.name: cypress-voice-plugin
plugins.description: Cypress plugin to announce spec result and time in Cypress Test Runner.

plugins.name: cypress-watch-and-reload
plugins.description: Reloads Cypress when one of the watched files changes.

plugins.name: vscode-cy-helper
plugins.description: Visual Studio Code extension for cypress and cucumber preprocessor.

plugins.name: Test Selection & Execution
plugins.description: Filter, tag, re-run, and control which tests execute and how failures are handled.

plugins.description: Filter tests using its title or tags.

plugins.name: cypress-cli-select
plugins.description: Cypress interactive cli prompt to select and run specific specs, tests or tags

plugins.description: Access runtime tags (CLI) and test tags (config) inside Cypress test logic to build context-aware conditional behavior.

plugins.description: A companion Cypress plugin for `cy-grep` that allows users to run specific test(s) in open mode.

plugins.name: cypress-plugin-last-failed
plugins.description: A companion Cypress plugin for `cy-grep` that re-runs the last failed test(s).

plugins.description: A Cypress plugin that enables faker functions in json fixture files.

plugins.description: Generate realistic test data for Cypress tests using Faker.js.

plugins.description: Cypress commands to query SQL Server, MySQL, OracleDB, and Postgres databases.

plugins.name: Accessibility

plugins.description: Measure duration of commands and different stages of cypress lifecycle. Log to terminal.

plugins.description: Measure performance in Cypress tests, keep a baseline in git, and catch regressions in CI.

plugins.name: cypress-timings
plugins.description: A Cypress plugin for reporting individual command timings.

plugins.description: Plugin to save code coverage collected during Cypress tests.
